@Caozhi It's been 2 month already. Today I also got this error. Have you found any solution?

I've also made a google search about this error and found one mention here: https://www.spinics.net/lists/kernel/msg4750171.html This was an email from 5 apr
Can I ask what distro are you guys on, and what version of rustc are you using?
And what is the target architecture ?

I ran into that, it happens with older versions of bindgen built against newer versions of LLVM/clang. @Miguel Ojeda we really need to update bindgen...

@Mark Lansky it should work if you use bindgen 0.62.0 (which is compatible with the kernel and fixes that LLVM issue)
We have discussed updating bindgen the other day, yeah. I also saw the issue when testing the compiler upgrade in a newer VM.
We should just bump bindgen to latest version.

Indeed, even if we do not track the latest, I would like to jump as farther as we can when we do jump. I will submit the bindgen upgrade this cycle. I have contacted bindgen regarding potentially being able to track bindgen from the kernel side, and testing the kernel on their side.

Asahi Lina said:

I ran into that, it happens with older versions of bindgen built against newer versions of LLVM/clang. Miguel Ojeda we really need to update bindgen...

Mark Lansky it should work if you use bindgen 0.62.0 (which is compatible with the kernel and fixes that LLVM issue)

I tried installing 0.62 but it failed because that version does not have bindgen binary. Did you have any additional changes to enable building with 0.62 ?
Use bindgen-cli for newer versions instead.

Just did a clean build, but I'm still having the error

***
*** Rust bindings generator 'bindgen' is too new. This may or may not work.
***   Your version:     0.62.0
***   Expected version: 0.56.0
***
***

*** version does not match Clang's. This may be a problem.

***   Clang version:    16.0.1
***
  RUSTC L rust/core.o
  BINDGEN rust/bindings/bindings_generated.rs
  BINDGEN rust/bindings/bindings_helpers_generated.rs
error: unknown argument: '-mindirect-branch-cs-prefix'
error: '-ftrivial-auto-var-init=zero' hasn't been enabled; enable it at your own peril for benchmarking purpose only with '-enable-trivial-auto-var-init-zero-knowing-it-will-be-removed-from-clang'

panicked at 'Unable to generate bindings: ClangDiagnostic("error: unknown argument: '-mindirect-branch-cs-prefix'\nerror: '-ftrivial-auto-var-init=zero' hasn't been enabled; enable it at your own peril for benchmarking purpose only with '-enable-trivial-auto-var-init-zero-knowing-it-will-be-removed-from-clang'\nerror: unknown warning option '-Wno-cast-function-type-strict'; did you mean '-Wno-cast-function-type'? [-Wunknown-warning-option]\n")', /home/aj/.cargo/registry/src/github.com-1ecc6299db9ec823/bindgen-cli-0.62.0/main.rs:52:36

panicked at 'Unable to generate bindings: ClangDiagnostic("error: unknown argument: '-mindirect-branch-cs-prefix'\nerror: '-ftrivial-auto-var-init=zero' hasn't been enabled; enable it at your own peril for benchmarking purpose only with '-enable-trivial-auto-var-init-zero-knowing-it-will-be-removed-from-clang'\nerror: unknown warning option '-Wno-cast-function-type-strict'; did you mean '-Wno-cast-function-type'? [-Wunknown-warning-option]\n")', /home/aj/.cargo/registry/src/github.com-1ecc6299db9ec823/bindgen-cli-0.62.0/main.rs:52:36
make[1]: *** [rust/Makefile:335: rust/bindings/bindings_generated.rs] Error 101
make[1]: *** Deleting file 'rust/bindings/bindings_generated.rs'
make: *** [Makefile:1292: prepare] Error 2
Do you have d966c3cab924 ("rust: allow to use INIT_STACK_ALL_ZERO"), which is in 6.3? Or what kernel are you compiling?
I also see-mindirect-branch-cs-prefix there, which should not be giving an error.
Are you using LLVM=1?

-mindirect-branch-cs-prefix was added in clang 16.0.0. I assume you're building with LLVM=1, and then that option doesn't get filtered from bindgen since it uses the clang options directly, but due to the version mismatch bindgen can't handle it. It looks like it only gets used on x86 with CONFIG_RETPOLINE, so maybe try disabling that? For the other one, make sure you have the patch Miguel mentioned or disable CONFIG_INIT_STACK_ALL_ZERO. For the last one, that's also probably caused by the mismatch, since the kernel decides what -Wno-* arguments to pass based on the clang version, and then that mismatches libclang.

@Miguel Ojeda It looks like LLVM=1 with a major version mismatch between libclang and clang is almost always going to fail given the way the kernel constructs C compiler arguments... I think we should make that diagnostic clearer, maybe adding something like "this will likely cause unknown argument errors". I don't think we can support those mismatches easily if we want to share C compiler options between clang and bindgen...
